purpose: Reduced sensitivity check (ex05 section 5.4 asks for a throughput-vs-output-length analysis; run_full_benchmark_suite's full 2-prompt x 3-length x 3-method grid was judged impractical -- see docs/PRD.md section 1.2 and README scope note -- so this is a second data point at max_new_tokens=16 for the fastest successful local method, Q4_0, to compare against the existing 30-token point in results/quantized_phi3_medium_q4_0.json).
prompt: Explain in one short sentence what virtual memory is.
generated_text: Virtual memory allows a computer to compensate for physical RAM shortages by temporarily
analysis: Compared to the 30-token run (tokens_per_sec=0.052, results/quantized_phi3_medium_q4_0.json), the 16-token run is SLOWER in aggregate throughput (0.04 tok/s) despite a similar per-token decode cost (tpot 15.2s vs 18.7s) -- because TTFT (a largely fixed per-request cost, ~21.7s here) is amortized over fewer output tokens. This is exactly the sensitivity effect ex05 section 5.4 asks to characterize: aggregate tokens/sec is not a constant property of the method, it depends on requested output length, because Prefill (TTFT) is a fixed cost while Decode (TPOT) scales with tokens generated.
